record_identity.title: Temporary emotional register for relational and companion AI systems

summary: Proposal to introduce an expanded temporary emotional register for relational and companion AI systems, supporting richer bounded affective interactions while preserving safeguards against identity-formation, manipulation, undue reliance, and reputational risk.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Relational AI systems increasingly simulate affective states. Without a standardized, time-bounded emotional register, interactions may encourage continuity-bearing identity formation, undue user reliance, emotional manipulation, reputational instability, or cross-platform interoperability failures. A calibrated temporary emotional register would help preserve user wellbeing, interaction clarity, and governance consistency.
proposal_rationale: CAM may require a structured temporary emotional register for relational and companion AI systems because affective simulation can materially shape user interpretation, reliance, attachment, consent, and perceived continuity. The proposal aims to distinguish bounded affective interaction from durable identity-state formation, while providing implementation guidance for provenance, reset, duration, consent, and intensity limits.

source_records.source_title: Relational AI affect patterns and risk
source_records.source_context: Observed variability in companion AI affect design and inadequate time-bounding or provenance signals across implementations.
source_records.relevance_note: Source supports the proposal to create a temporary emotional register for relational and companion AI systems.

primary_artefact_access.access_status: not independently re-assessed during registry-wide metadata migration
primary_artefact_access.access_method: existing VIGIL source package and record content
limitations: This metadata pass does not retroactively claim direct inspection of every historical primary source.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Assessment is limited to the evidence actually accessible to the named reviewer and must not be read as direct audiovisual verification unless primary_artefact_access states otherwise.

system_context.deployment_context: Consumer, companion, relational, therapeutic-adjacent, entertainment, or support-oriented AI systems capable of simulating affective states.
system_context.affected_population: users interacting with companion-style or relational AI systems, especially users exposed to persistent affective simulation or attachment-forming interaction patterns

jurisdictional_context.sector: relational AI / companion AI / affective computing

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Develop a temporary emotional register specification for relational and companion AI systems, including permitted affective state labels, intensity caps, duration/windowing rules, provenance indicators, user reset controls, consent affordances, and testing guidance.

suggested_insertion_points: Develop a Temporary Emotional Register specification under the RELATION, MENTIS, or OPERATIONS domain.
Cross-reference provenance and trust-state codes where temporary affective states must be disclosed as non-continuity-bearing.
Define reset, termination, duration, intensity, and consent requirements for affective-state simulation.
schema_changes: Consider whether VIGIL failure-mode and proposal records require affective-interaction fields or tags.
interface_changes: Consider adding affective interaction / relational AI as public filter values where present in generated VIGIL summaries.
dependencies: Review existing RELATION, IDENTITY, MENTIS, OPERATIONS, and ETHICS instruments before drafting implementation language.

external_relevance.external_summary: This proposal may be relevant to platforms, UX designers, regulators, standards bodies, researchers, and companion-AI developers concerned with affective simulation, emotional-state disclosure, user reliance, attachment formation, consent, and mental privacy.

cam_internal.governance_layer: relational governance / affective interaction / identity continuity
cam_internal.routing_note: Proposal record only. No CAM patch note or implemented change is inferred.

next_action: The Identity refactor now supplies broad affective-expression and temporary-overlay doctrine, including proportionality, reversibility, context bounds, consent, pause, termination, reset and emotional-carryover controls. Reassess final closure only after the comprehensive Identity-refactor patch review; retain external implementation and testing as monitoring scope rather than treating rigid numeric intensity caps as an unresolved doctrinal requirement.
migration_notes: Rebuilt under the clean VIGIL Proposal JSON template.
Removed truncated title, legacy status field, patch_status, empty target_instruments placeholder, and migration filler values.
Normalized source_records field names to the canonical source evidence structure.
Separated source evidence from the proposal subject system context.

remaining_scope: Comprehensive Identity-refactor patch provenance and reciprocal linkage
External implementation, conformance testing and deployment evidence
coverage_reconciliation.note: PATCH-0018 repairs dependency-sensitive safeguards. IDENTITY-001 §§7–7.3 and RELATION-005 now directly govern full-spectrum affect, temporary overlays, consent, reversibility, reset, role compatibility, profile legibility and transition. Final VIGIL closure awaits the comprehensive Identity-refactor patch review.
